courthouse.def: 法院大樓、郡政府所在地人物 (單數) (法)

courtier.def: 朝臣、弄臣、討好者人物 (單數) (臣)
courtier.ex: The royal courtier bowed to the queen.

courtly.ex: He bowed in a very courtly manner.

court-martial.def: 軍事法庭、軍事法庭的人物、召開軍法審判的人的人人物 (單數) (軍)
court-martial.ex: He had to face a public court-martial.

courtroom.def: 法庭、審判室人物 (單數) (審)
courtroom.ex: The courtroom was very quiet today.

courtship.def: 求愛、求婚、求愛期間人物 (單數) (愛)
courtship.ex: Their long courtship lasted ten years.

courtyard.ex: The hotel has a beautiful inner courtyard.

cousin.def: 堂(表)兄弟姊妹、親戚人物 (單數) (親)
cousin.col: first cousin, second cousin, distant cousin
cousin.ex: He is my first cousin on my father's side.

couture.def: 時裝、高級時裝業、縫紉的人的人的人物 (單數) (裝)
couture.ex: She loves wearing expensive haute couture.

couturier.def: 女裝設計師、時裝店主人人物 (單數) (設)
couturier.ex: The expert couturier made her dress.

cove.def: 小海灣、山凹、(口)傢伙的人的人人物 (單數) (灣)
cove.ex: The boat lay in a small sheltered cove.

covenant.def: 盟約、契約、立約的人的人人物 (單數) (約)
covenant.ex: They signed a binding legal covenant.

cover.def: 覆蓋、掩護、險種、報導的人的人人物 (單數) (蓋)
cover.col: under cover, cover distance, insurance cover
cover.ex: The forest provided good local cover.

coverage.def: 報導、險種、覆蓋範圍人物 (單數) (報)
coverage.col: press coverage, media coverage, insurance coverage
coverage.ex: The event received lot of media coverage.

covered.def: 覆蓋的、隱蔽的人的人 (或物) (蓋)
covered.ex: The mountain was snow-covered all year.

covering.def: 覆蓋物、掩護的人 (或物) (外)
covering.ex: The floor covering was old and worn.

coverlet.ex: She made an embroidered coverlet for bed.

covers.def: 餐位、封面、被子人物 (複數使用) (蓋)
covers.ex: The book has ten thousand covers sold.

covert.def: 隱密的、遮蔽處、隱居處的人物、隱蔽的人的人人物 (單數) (隱)
covert.ex: They carried out a secret covert operation.

covertly.ex: He acted covertly to help his friend.

covet.def: 渴望、垂涎、貪圖的人的人
covet.ex: Don't covet your neighbor's new car.

covetous.ex: He cast covetous eyes on the gems.

covey.def: 小群、(鳥)一群的人物 (單數) (群)
covey.ex: A small covey of quail flew away.

cow.def: 母牛、乳牛、恐嚇、威脅的人的人人物 (單數) (牛)
cow.col: cash cow (搖錢樹), sacred cow (神聖不可侵犯者)
cow.ex: The new product is a real cash cow now.

coward.def: 懦夫、膽怯地人物、膽怯的人的人人物 (單數) (懦)
coward.ex: The man was a miserable local coward.

cowardice.def: 赤子之心、膽怯、懦弱的人物 (單數) (懦)
cowardice.ex: He was accused of extreme moral cowardice.

cowardly.def: 懦弱的、膽怯地的人的人物 (單數) (懦)
cowardly.ex: That was a very cowardly attack on kids.

cow-boy/cowboy.def: 牛仔、牧牛工、冒失的人人物 (單數) (牛)
cow-boy/cowboy.ex: He loves watching old cowboy movies.

cowed.def: 被嚇倒的、威懾的人的人 (或物) (嚇)
cowed.ex: The children were in a cowed silence.

cowhand.def: 牧牛工、牛仔人物 (單數) (牛)
cowhand.ex: The experienced cowhand knew the way.

cowhide.def: 牛皮的人物、鞭打的人的人人物 (單數) (皮)
cowhide.ex: The boots are made of thick local cowhide.

cowl.def: 兜帽、帽型煙囪帽的人的人人物 (單數) (帽)
cowl.ex: The monk pulled his cowl over his head.

cowlick.def: 額前的一綹變髮、亂髮人物 (單數) (髮)
cowlick.ex: He had a stubborn cowlick on his head.

cowpox.ex: Cowpox was used to prevent smallpox.

cowpuncher.ex: The lonely cowpuncher rode all night.

cowry/cowrie.ex: The shiny cowry shell was on beach.

cowslip.ex: Yellow cowslips grew in the green meadow.

cox.def: 舵手、指揮舵手的人的人人物 (單數) (舵)
cox.ex: The rowing cox shouted to the team.

coxcomb.def: 花花公子、紈褲子弟、公雞冠人物 (單數) (貴)
coxcomb.ex: He is a vain and foolish local coxcomb.

coxswain.def: 舵手的人物、當舵手的人的人人物 (單數) (舵)
coxswain.ex: The boat's coxswain steered the boat.

coy.ex: She gave him a very sweet and coy smile.

coyly.ex: She laughed coyly at his compliments.

coyness.def: 羞怯、靦腆的人物 (單數) (羞)
coyness.ex: Her natural coyness was very charming.

coyote.def: 土狼、叢林狼人物 (單數) (狼)
coyote.ex: The howling coyote woke up the camp.

cozen.def: 欺騙、騙取、瞞騙的人的人
cozen.ex: They managed to cozen him into a deal.

crab.def: 螃蟹、螃蟹肉、脾氣乖戾的人、糾纏的人的人人物 (單數) (蟹)
crab.col: crab cake, crab apple, catch a crab (划槳失誤)
crab.ex: He caught a crab and fell from the boat.

crabbed.def: 脾氣乖戾的、字跡潦草的人的人 (或物) (壞)
crabbed.ex: His crabbed handwriting was hard to read.

crabby.ex: He was a very crabby and old teacher.

crack.def: 裂縫、爆裂聲、解碼、最好的的人物、最好的人的人人物 (單數) (裂)
crack.col: crack a joke, crack down on (打擊), at the crack of dawn
crack.ex: We set off at the crack of dawn today.

crackdown.def: 鎮壓、嚴厲打擊人物 (單數) (打)
crackdown.ex: The police launched a severe crackdown.

cracked.def: 破裂的、(口)瘋狂地的人的人 (或物) (破)
cracked.ex: He sang in a very old and cracked voice.

cracker.def: 薄脆餅、鞭炮、解碼者、(口)白人窮鬼人物 (單數) (餅)
cracker.ex: He ate a salty cheese cracker for lunch.

crackers.def: (口)瘋狂的人的人的人物 (單數) (狂)
crackers.ex: This loud noise is driving me crackers.

cracking.def: 破碎、傑出的的人 (或物) (傑)
cracking.col: cracking pace, get cracking (開始行動)
cracking.ex: Let's get cracking or we'll be late today.

crackle.def: 發碎裂聲、龜裂的人的人人物 (單數) (碎)
crackle.ex: The dry wood gave a loud sharp crackle.

crackpot.def: (口)古怪的人、瘋子人物 (單數) (奇)
crackpot.ex: He came up with another crackpot theory.

cradle.def: 搖籃、發祥地、護架、搖的人的人人物 (單數) (搖)
cradle.col: from the cradle to the grave, cradle of civilization
cradle.ex: Greece is the cradle of civilization now.

craft.def: 工藝、手藝、船、飛行器、精心製作的人的人人物 (單數) (藝)
craft.col: landing craft, aircraft, spacecraft, arts and crafts
craft.ex: She teaches local arts and crafts today.

craftily.ex: He replied craftily to all the questions.

craftiness.def: 狡猾、巧妙的人物 (單數) (猾)
craftiness.ex: His natural craftiness won him the win.

craftsman.ex: The skilled craftsman made the chair.
